text:
The waiting is over and the 2024 SummerOlympicsare here, with all eyes focussed onParisfor its first Games in 100 years.

Spectators have already been treated to plenty of action after the football, rugby sevens, handball and shooting competitions got underway in the week.

But last night’sspectacular – if soggy – opening ceremony well and truly kick-started the greatest sporting event of them alland the stage is set for what promises to be a fortnight of compelling drama involving over 10,000 athletes from 206 nations across the globe, so buckle up…

Team GB’s first medals were clinched by diving duo Yasmin Harper and Scarlett Mew Jensen (bronze) and cyclist Anna Henderson (silver) on Saturday.

European time trial champion Josh Tarling brutally missed out on a medal after suffering a puncture, though, with the Welshman finishing down in fourth place.

There were amusing scenes in the tennis asone player offered a fan to take his placewhen he lost eight games in a row to Novak Djokovic. But what else is going on today? We’ll be across it all here atMetro.co.ukwith our live blog…

This blog is now closed. For the latest updates, visitMetro’s Sport page.

That’s it for Day 1 of Paris 2024as Team GB got off the mark with a silver and a bronze medal.

The action will begin on Sunday at 8.30am and there are plenty more opportunities for Great Britain to boost their medal count.

Penny HealeyandBryony Pitmanwill start in the archery at 8:30am with the gold-medal match at 16:11pm.

Helen Glover, Team GB’s Olympic rowing champion in 2012 and 2016, returns for her fourth Olympics and is joined byRebecca Shorten,Esme BoothandSam Redgravein the women’s four heats from 11:30am.

Team GB begin open their women’s hockey group-stage campaign against Spain at 12:15pm.

Evie Richardsis back for the women’s cross-country mountain bike event from 13:10pm.

The British women’s rugby sevens play Ireland in their opening group game at 14:30pm.

Kimberley Woodsis in the canoe slalom’s K1 event which begins at 14:30pm, while the final is at 16:45pm.

Chelsie Giles, who won bronze in the 52kg women’s judo in Tokyo, is back again at Paris 2024. The women’s medal contests begin at 16:49pm.

Adam Peatywill be in the final of the men’s 100m breaststroke at 20:54pm.

We will be updating you once again tomorrow, have a lovely evening.

The identity of the mystery Olympic torchbearer has finally been revealed.

Full story here…

Team GB’sAdam Peatyhas won themen’s 100m breaststroke semi finalswith an impressive performance.

He finished ahead of Nic Fink of the United States, the current world champion.

Canadahave received a six-point deduction and their women’s football coach has been banned byFIFAfor 12 months over the drone spying scandal at theParisOlympics.

Click here for the full story.



FrenchUFCstar Benoit Saint-Denis hit out at theOlympicopening ceremony,claiming that Christianity was ‘humiliated’.

Team GB cycling starJosh Tarlingcouldn’t stop ‘swearing’ after being robbed of an Olympic gold medal at Paris 2024.

Click here for the full story.

Samoa boxing coach Lionel Elika Fatupaitohas died aged 60 after suffering a cardiac arrest in the Olympic village on Friday.

Fatupaito was with an athlete when he fell ill in his room at around 10:20 a.m. local time on Friday morning, just hours before the opening ceremony inParis.

France’s Shirine Boukli has won bronze after beating Spain’s Laura Martinez in the 48kg women’s judo.

We’ve had medals awarded in three events so far, starting in the10m air rifle mixed team eventwhere China’s duo of Huang Yuting and Sheng Lihao won gold.🥇Sheng/Huang (CHN)🥈Keum/Park (KOR)🥉Le/Satpayev (KAZ)

China added to their gold medal tally with Chang Yani and Chen Yiwen winning thewomen’s 3m synchronised 3m springboard.

Team GB got their first medal of Paris 2024 with Yasmin Harper and Scarlett Mew Jensen winning bronze.

🥇Chang/Chen (CHN) – 337.68🥈Bacon/Cook (USA) – 314.64🥉Harper/Mew Jensen (GBR) – 302.28

In thewomen’s individual time trial cycling, Australia’s Grace Brown secured gold, while Team GB debutant Anna Henderson won silver.

🥇 Grace Brown (AUS) 39:38.24🥈 Anna Henderson (GBR) +1:31.59🥉 Chloe Dygert (USA) +1:32.46

It’s not just the competitors having trouble with the rain! ð§#BBCOlympics#Olympics#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/uAU4tVpmCy

Just like the the women’s individual time trial, the men are being forced to deal with tricky, wet conditions.

‘I hope we don’t see any bad crashes – I hope we don’t see any crashes – but I am expecting maybe one or two,’ Commonwealth cycling bronze medallist Hayley Simmonds told the BBC.

‘We could see our first GB gold medal in this one. I really believe Josh Tarling has got a really strong chance of bringing home a gold medal. He is an incredible talent.’

Great Britain have two medals so far – one bronze and one silver.

Josh Tarling is now aiming to register Team GB’s first gold medal in the men’s individual time trial.

The pre-race favourite sets off at16.15UK time.

The men will take on the same 32.4km route as the women, from Pont Alexandre III into the Bois de Vincennes and back.

Great Britain’s SILVER medallist Anna Henderson on her final stretch in the women’s road cycling time trials ð¥ð´ââï¸What a performance!#BBCOlympics#Olympics#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/4cg6IHtVla

‘I can’t believe it,’ Anna Henderson told Eurosport after landing the silver medal, in what is the second overall medal for Team GB at Paris 2024.

‘I had a small feeling I might be able to reach the podium today, squeeze out some of the other riders, but I can’t believe I’ve come second behind someone like Grace. It’s unbelievable.

ð£ “I burst into tears when I found out!” ð¥ºJust looks what it means to Anna Henderson to become an Olympic silver medallist ðð¥#Paris2024#Olympicspic.twitter.com/3f6nS2sMMT

‘I burst into tears when I found out I was second. I can’t stop smiling, I’m over the moon.

‘I just focused on power in the straights and caution in the corners … I thought I could lose a whole Olympic Games there. I saw a lot of girls have problems today, so really happy I could hold it upright.’

All smiles for Team GB’s second medallist at Paris 2024, Anna Henderson! A really gusty ride from the 25-year-old, who is making her Olympic debut.

Team GB’s Anna Henderson has won Great Britain’s second Olympic medal at Paris 2024 with a silver in the women’s individual time trial!

The Brit overcame tricky, rainy conditions to finish in 41 minutes and nine seconds – a result which no-one was really predicting.

America’s Chloe Dygert fell less than a second short of Henderson’s time to end up in third place. Grace Brown of Australia took the gold.

The falls just keep on coming! ð¦USA’s Taylor Knibb, and her team mechanic, had falls in the women’s road cycling time trials.#BBCOlympics#Olympics#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/vrLDa1Kvos

The rain is causing chaos over in the women’s individual time trial at Paris 2024, where Team GB’s Anna Henderson is in action.

‘This is such a tricky course, the rain making all the difference on a street circuit,’ Chris Boardman told the BBC.

The Olympic cycling champion added: ‘I hope they get a marshal on that corner since we have had two riders go down on the same spot.

‘Judgement is all part of game. It isn’t just power, it isn’t brute force, it’s also technique and judgement and knowing where to back off.’

TheOlympic tennis is underway… but there is no Emma Raducanu for Team GB.

She recently posted on her Instagram story that she is in Washington for the for the Mubadala Citi DC Open.

Here’s why the British star is not featuring at Paris 2024.

Olympic tennis star Matthew Ebden begged a fan to replace him during his brutal first-round singles defeat to Novak Djokovic at Paris 2024 on Saturday.

Click here for the full story.

Don’t think. Just do.

“Don’t think, just do” ðªJake Jarman’s motto paid off earlier today!#BBCOlympics#Olympics#Paris2024pic.twitter.com/hOEVptpZ9n

Britain’s male gymnasts enjoyed a strong morning in qualification!

Max Whitlock told the BBC: ‘It was about taking it step by step. Starting on the vault, the boys started amazing, I think the atmosphere lifted us. To be honest, in qualification, when you round it up, it’s the hardest one by an absolute mile.

‘Everyone goes in feeling like they know when the pressure’s going to hit, where their specialist pieces are, and the tension builds when it gets to that, but when it hits, the feeling’s unbelievable. Finishing the way the boys did, I think we can all be very, very proud. But it’s only step one.

‘I don’t think we can be happy, everyone ticked the boxes we needed to and everyone did their job amazingly, so we can use this to build confidence. We’ve still got a big job to do.’

ArrowMORE:Every Team GB medal winner from Paris Olympics 2024

title: Olympic Games 2024 live: Adam Peaty through to final as Team GB end Day 1 with two medals | Metro News
description: Follow the latest action and live news from the 2024 Olympic Games in Paris as swimming, volleyball, dressage, tennis and more get underway.
